pi_items.source: Code verification: setup_task.sh line 30, export_result.sh lines 14, 22, 45. File path is hardcoded and used for data validation.
Code verification: export_result.sh line 110 implements 60 <= hr_val <= 85. However, task.json description line 5 states 'Heart Rate 60-80 BPM' for baseline. Metadata contradicts description.

Code verification: export_result.sh line 111 implements 95 <= spo2_val <= 100. However, task.json description line 5 states 'SpO2 96-100%' for baseline. Medical standard (from Cleveland Clinic, Pulmonary sources) confirms 95-100% is normal. Metadata contradicts description.
Code verification: export_result.sh line 120 implements hr_val > 115. However, task.json description line 5 states 'Heart Rate > 120 BPM' for distress. Metadata contradicts description.

Code verification: export_result.sh line 121 implements spo2_val < 92. However, task.json description line 5 states 'SpO2 < 90%' for distress. Medical standard defines hypoxemia as SpO2 < 90% (Open Critical Care, Mayo Clinic, Cleveland Clinic sources). Metadata contradicts both description and medical definition.

privileged_info_summary: The task operationalizes hypoxic tachycardia detection using the OpenICE simulator. Generated data will be in CSV format at /home/ga/Desktop/hypoxia_test_data.csv. Baseline physiological state is detected when Heart Rate is 60-85 BPM OR SpO2 is 95-100%. Distress state is detected when Heart Rate exceeds 115 BPM OR SpO2 drops below 92%. These verification thresholds are hardcoded into the verifier script (export_result.sh lines 110-121). However, all four metadata thresholds directly contradict the requirements stated in the task description (which specifies HR 60-80, SpO2 96-100 for baseline; HR >120, SpO2 <90 for distress), and the distress_spo2_max of 92 contradicts the clinical definition of hypoxemia (<90%).
